locations.name: Far North Queensland
locations.summary: Far North Queensland (FNQ) is the northernmost part of the Australian state of Queensland (QLD).

locations.name: 新南威爾士州
locations.summary: 新南威爾斯州（英語：New South Wales，缩写为NSW），或譯紐修威省，简称新州、紐省，是澳洲人口最多的一州；位於澳洲的東南部，昆士兰州以南、维多利亞州以北，南澳州以東，首府在悉尼，境內包圍整個澳洲首都領地。新南威爾斯今劃分為1…

locations.name: 維多利亞州
locations.summary: 维多利亚州（英語：Victoria，缩写为VIC），简称維州、維省，又譯域多利省，是澳大利亞東岸南部的一个州，是澳洲大陸本土五个联邦州中幅員最小的（僅237629平方公里），但人口卻為澳洲第二多（接近670万，僅次於新南威爾斯州），人口密…

locations.name: 南澳大利亚州
locations.summary: 南澳大利亚州（英語：South Australia，缩写为SA），簡稱南澳或南澳州，位於澳大利亚中南部，与澳大利亚大陆的其餘四州及北領地接壤，是澳洲聯邦的一州，是唯一与澳洲大陸上所有聯邦州都接壤的一州，其下劃分為69個地方政府區域。 南澳…

locations.name: 阿德莱德
locations.summary: 阿德莱德（英語：Adelaide，/ˈædɪleɪd/；考尔纳语：Tarndanya，發音：[ˈd̪̥aɳɖaɲa]）是澳大利亚聯邦南澳大利亚州的首府和也是该州第一大城、商业和文化中心，澳大利亚第五大城，人口逾141万。77%的南澳洲居民…

locations.name: 大分水岭
locations.summary: 大分水岭（英語：Great Dividing Range），也称东部高地（Eastern Highlands），亦音譯作大狄維亭山脈，是澳大利亚大陆东部沿岸大致与海岸线平行的山脉、丘陵和高原的总称，北起约克角半岛，南至维多利亚州西部，长3…

relations.subject: Noisy miner
relations.evidence: Found in a broad arc from Far North Queensland through New South Wales and Victoria to Tasmania and southeastern South Australia, and out to far north-west Queensland, the noisy miner primarily inhabits dry, open eucalypt forests that lack understory shrubs

relations.subject: Noisy miner
relations.evidence: Its range in South Australia has been steadily expanding since it was first recorded near Adelaide in the early 1890s. It is sedentary over its entire range. The noisy miner is territorial, and the territory of a colony is aggressively defended—which has led to a significant reduction in avian diversity in areas occupied by the noisy miner, with smaller species excluded. thumb|right|Open eucalypt forests without understory shrubs suit the noisy miner. thumb|Noisy miner (Manorina melanocephala), Melbourne, Australia The noisy miner primarily inhabits dry, open eucalypt forest without understory shrubs

relations.subject: Noisy miner
relations.evidence: It is commonly found in open sclerophyll forests, including those on coastal dunes or granite outcrops; forests dominated by spotted gum on mountain ridges and exposed slopes; box and ironbark forests on the foothills of the Great Dividing Range; mixed forests of eucalypts and cypress (Callitris); forests dominated by yapunya, mulga, gidgee, brigalow or emu bush; in stands of belah and scattered clumps of boree; on the edges of woodlands of river red gum, including swamp woodlands bordering floodplains, and areas dominated by exotic species, such as European ash and willow
